facts.fact: Agent means a task-assignable abstraction of a configured runtime
facts.human_doc_phrase: Agent means a task-assignable abstraction of a configured runtime

downstreams.local_scope: HTTP handlers, stores, mock data, and generated client projection
runtime execution after assignment
deployment requirement interpretation only

facts.fact: Agent editability requires zero assigned tasks
facts.human_doc_phrase: Agent editability requires zero assigned tasks

source_refs.required_phrase: An agent is editable only when it has no assigned tasks

downstreams.local_scope: editability projection and HTTP write rejection
button state and user-facing affordance

facts.fact: DSL to IR to OpenAPI projection rules

downstreams.local_scope: checked-in OpenAPI copy, mock server behavior, and generated client handoff
generated TypeScript/React Query consumer surface

facts.fact: Generated client delivery PRs are review handoffs and must not be auto-merged by delivery automation
facts.human_doc_phrase: Generated client delivery PR review handoff

source_refs.required_phrase: Generated client delivery PRs are review handoffs

downstreams.local_scope: generated branch creation, PR creation, release manifest, and path allowlist execution

facts.fact: Generated client path searchability is derived from client.module and client.facade_path

source_refs.required_phrase: Generated client path searchability is also a contract projection fact

downstreams.local_scope: generated comments expose the full generated access path and module-local search path
frontend developers search generated files by paths such as tasks.threadMessages.create

facts.fact: instruction is optional client-authored provider-neutral agent guidance text capped at 1000 characters and snapshotted into Assignment.agent_instruction at assignment creation
facts.human_doc_phrase: `instruction` is optional client-authored provider-neutral agent guidance text capped at 1000 characters

downstreams.local_scope: PATCH validation, save/update behavior, response projection, and Assignment.agent_instruction snapshotting
provider-specific placement/effectiveness harnesses from Assignment.agent_instruction
no Terraform diff unless future secret/media/durability policy appears

facts.fact: Participant dropdown agent ordering is owned-first, display-name ordered, then agent_id tied
facts.human_doc_phrase: Participant dropdown agent ordering is owned-first, display-name ordered, then `agent_id` tied

downstreams.local_scope: assignable-agent response sorting and black-box HTTP coverage
participant dropdown renders the returned order and handles long labels locally

facts.fact: profile_thumbnail_url is an optional HTTPS image URL string
facts.human_doc_phrase: `profile_thumbnail_url` is an optional HTTPS image URL string

downstreams.local_scope: PATCH validation, save/update behavior, and response projection
avatar rendering
ignored; not injected into native config
no Terraform diff until hosted media storage is required

facts.fact: Admin, owner, public, and private visibility vocabulary

downstreams.local_scope: executable RBAC evaluator and request authorization
visible list and edit/delete affordance
assignment payload carries the instruction value
runtime config only after infra publishes deployment inputs

facts.fact: AI Agent v2 client API is scoped by workspace_id path parameters while workspace list/create remains outside the AI Agent contract

downstreams.local_scope: v2 route parsing, workspace-scoped mock store records, authorization context, and persistence schema version marker
generated calls such as riido.v2.aiAgent.agents.create with workspace_id params

repo_dependencies.local_scope: Generated React Query code and API history are delivered by the control-plane workflow.
Control-plane executes HTTP, mock, authorization, and generated-client behavior without redefining canonical policy.
Daemon consumes assigned agent settings only at runtime boundaries.
Infra decides whether contract facts require topology, secret, media, or evidence changes.
